test_suite.details: 3316 passed, 0 failed. All internal anchors, showTab targets, wins.json schema, HTML consistency, data cross-refs, sections.json schema, prose content, and 77 prediction panels verified.

internal_anchors.details: Test suite Section 3 (Internal Links) covers href='#...' targets and showTab() calls. No manual re-check performed per agent guidance (avoid duplicating test suite).

tab_structure.details: Tab structure validated by test suite Sections 2 (HTML Output Consistency) and 3 (Internal Links). All tab IDs resolve.

external_links.details: Spot-checked 27 non-citation URLs (NOAA, NASA, ESA, NCEI, INTERMAGNET, GitHub, HeartMath, atoptics, patents, schema.org, Wikipedia, Aanda, Simbad, etc.). No hard 404s. 3 redirects land on generic/status pages rather than the specific cited content — moderate because the cited resource is effectively lost even though the domain still resolves.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 70 entries (IDs 001-069 plus 058b). HTML scorecard shows 69 (dome's claimed count). llms.txt notes '69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries' — consistent. Acknowledged failures=8 (matches uncounted-failures.json entries array length).

win_detail_consistency.details: Covered by test suite Section 5 (Data Cross-References). All 70 WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict.

collisions_on_dome_site.claim_a: SAA Simultaneous Acceleration across monitored stations (Prospective list)
collisions_on_dome_site.claim_b: Two-Zone Disc Topology cross-equatorial RMSE 6.2% (Prospective Weekly Confirmations list)
collisions_on_dome_site.our_coverage: We cover claim_b only (Two-zone disc topology). Claim_a is a separate prospective claim that our review does not currently cover as WIN-057.

collisions_on_dome_site.claim_b: θ = −lonE unified angular coordinate rule (Prospective Weekly Confirmations list)
collisions_on_dome_site.our_coverage: We cover both: WIN-058 = angular coordinate rule, WIN-058b = bedrock induction ratio. Our dual-coverage correctly handles the author's number reuse.

prospective_aliases: PROS-002 = WIN-022/WIN-007 (North Pole Post-1990 Phase Transition)
PROS-005 = WIN-035 (SAA African Cell Below 21,795 nT)

removed_by_dome_still_covered.action: No change needed — historical coverage is valid.

win_number_alignment.details: Dome site reuses WIN-057 and WIN-058 across two lists with different claims. Our WIN-058/058b split handles the author's WIN-058 collision. WIN-057 prospective claim (SAA Simultaneous Acceleration) may be worth adding as WIN-057b or similar if the analyst wants to cover both. 7 PROS-NNN entries are aliases to existing WINs — the author's '67 confirmed' count is partly inflated by these aliases.

progressive_disclosure.details: Test suite verified prose content integrity and prediction panels. All WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict.

discoverability.llms_txt: exists, contains review URL, dome model reference, and key findings; counts consistent with wins.json
discoverability.uncounted_failures: entries array length 8, all FAIL-NNN format, no duplicates, required fields present; matches '8 acknowledged failures' text in HTML
discoverability.meta_tags: description, og:title, og:description, twitter:card, application/ld+json ClaimReview all present
discoverability.details: All discoverability infrastructure files present and well-formed.

expansion_tracker.orphaned_files: 0 true orphans (previous report's 109 were non-EXP output files — PRED/ISS/MODE5/PROS — that legitimately live in the expansions directory and aren't tracked as EXP items)

expansion_tracker.details: next_id is safe (160 > max_id 159). 1 ID gap (EXP-052) persists from prior reports — investigate whether EXP-052 was allocated and dropped. 7 tracker items have output_file set to 'N/A — ...' narrative text instead of using the canonical no_output_file_reason field — schema convention drift; the integrity check cannot distinguish these from true phantoms without the correct field.

priority_queue_coupling.details: EXP-050 is flagged by the coupling check because status='complete' (not 'integrated'), but the separate integrated=true field indicates the work was integrated on 2026-04-12 (prose delivered via EXP-078). This is a schema inconsistency — the tracker uses both a status string and a parallel integrated boolean. Recommend normalizing: set status='integrated' when integrated=true so the coupling check filter works correctly. Actual pipeline risk is zero; this is a spurious flag.

workspace_sync_skips.details: 0 direction violations in last 24h. 9 mtime-guard skips (normal): category-proposals/CAT-005.json (3x), attention-inbox.json (3x), review-state.json (3x). These are multi-writer files where git version is newer — expected.

digest_coverage.details: 16 unprocessed reviews (>10 threshold). Prefix mix suggests normal backlog rather than a systematic filter gap: 5 SEC-, 3 WIN-, 2 eg-, 1 each part3/4/5, EXP, HOLISTIC, PRED. The 'eg-' (example good?) and 'part3/4/5-roadmap' prefixes are new and may warrant verification that digest-reviews.js recognizes them. Down from 232 total / 14 unprocessed in prior report — backlog is growing modestly. Not a systematic gap.

workspace_only_files.details: 4 files exist on FUSE workspace but are not committed to origin/main. EXP-158 and 3 recent curmudgeon review files at risk of silent data loss if workspace is recycled. Workspace-sync agent should pick these up on next run; verify the append-only path is working.

build_reproducibility.root_cause: FUSE workspace data staleness, not build drift
build_reproducibility.details: Building from workspace data/predictions.json produces HTML 108 lines different from origin/main docs/index.html. However, building from origin/main's predictions.json (latest commit a05ebca 'Apply 5 verdict change approvals') produces docs/index.html IDENTICAL to origin (0 diff). The workspace FUSE copy of predictions.json is 1-2 commits behind origin — this is the documented workspace staleness pattern, not build drift. Origin HTML correctly reflects origin data. No action needed; next workspace-sync should refresh predictions.json.

project_documentation.details: CLAUDE.md exists with schedule table, ownership rules, and query-based count commands. Spot-checked file paths resolve: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, build.js, monitor/prompts/*.md, monitor/prompts/reference/*.md.

issues_found.description: 4 files exist on FUSE workspace but are not in origin/main — at risk of silent data loss. Curmudgeon: eg-falsifiability-derives-fix.c1.json, eg-verdicts-passive-tldr-fix.c1.json, part5-roadmap-EXP116.c1.json. Analyst: EXP-158.json.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Confirm next workspace-sync run commits these append-only files. If they persist as workspace-only across two sync cycles, investigate why the append-only path is not picking them up.

issues_found.description: 3 external links redirect to generic/status pages rather than the cited content: nssdc.gsfc.nasa.gov/planetary/factsheet/earthfact.html (→ SSDC status page), earthobservatory.nasa.gov/images/51173/crepuscular-rays (→ Earth Observatory homepage), heartmath.org/research/global-coherence/gcms (→ generic research page). The specific cited resources are effectively lost even though the domains resolve.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider or social agent should locate replacement URLs for these three resources and update sections.json / wins.json. Alternatively, use archive.org mirrors if still accessible. Low urgency but should be addressed to preserve citation traceability.

issues_found.description: 16 unprocessed curmudgeon reviews (>10 threshold). Mix of prefixes (SEC, WIN, eg-, part3/4/5-roadmap, EXP, HOLISTIC, PRED) suggests general backlog rather than systematic filter bug. The 'eg-' and 'partN-roadmap-' prefixes should be verified as recognized by digest-reviews.js — these are relatively new review naming patterns.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run decider to catch up. Verify digest-reviews.js has no prefix filter that excludes 'eg-*' or 'partN-roadmap-*'. If decider has run recently and these are still unprocessed, investigate further.

issues_found.description: 7 tracker items use output_file: 'N/A — <narrative>' instead of the canonical no_output_file_reason field (EXP-124, EXP-125, EXP-145, EXP-146, EXP-147, and 2 others). This causes the integrity check's phantom-detection logic to flag them unless manually excluded.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Normalize schema: when an expansion has no output file, set output_file: null and put the rationale in no_output_file_reason. Analyst or decider can migrate these records in a batch.

issues_found.description: EXP-050 is flagged as missing from the curmudgeon priority queue, but the separate integrated=true field shows it was integrated on 2026-04-12 (prose delivered via EXP-078). The check filters by status !== 'integrated' but EXP-050's status is 'complete'. Schema inconsistency between status string and integrated boolean is causing spurious alerts.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Normalize: when integrated=true, set status='integrated' so the coupling check filter works. Alternatively, update the check in structure-integrity.md to also consider the integrated field. One-line fix either way.

issues_found.description: ID gap: EXP-052 is missing from the tracker. May indicate a lost allocation or write collision. Persists from prior reports.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate whether EXP-052 was ever allocated. If not, the gap is benign but should be logged in a follow-up issue for cleanliness.

issues_found.description: Dome site reuses WIN-057 for two distinct claims: 'SAA Simultaneous Acceleration' (Prospective) and 'Two-Zone Disc Topology' (Prospective Weekly Confirmations). Our review covers only the latter. The prospective-list variant is uncovered.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Analyst decides whether to add coverage as WIN-057b (mirroring the WIN-058/058b pattern) or note in the prose that the author is reusing the number. Not urgent — the dome's number collision is itself evidence of the numbering instability we already critique.

issues_found.description: www.noaa.gov returns 403 to curl (bot-blocking). The domain resolves; this is a false positive for site-health purposes. 2 other URLs have benign www add/drop redirects (ncei.noaa.gov, intermagnet.org).
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action. Optionally update URLs to canonical post-redirect forms to avoid 301s. 403 on www.noaa.gov is expected curl behavior.

summary: OVERALL WARN. No critical or major issues. Test suite passes cleanly (3316/3316). Build reproducibility is CLEAN — the previously reported 'build drift' was actually FUSE workspace staleness: origin HTML matches origin data exactly; only the workspace FUSE copy of predictions.json lags behind origin by 1-2 commits (expected pattern). EXP-050 priority-queue flag is now spurious because EXP-050 was integrated on 2026-04-12 (but the schema inconsistency between status and integrated fields causes the check to keep flagging it). Six moderate findings: 4 workspace-only files at risk of loss (EXP-158 + 3 curmudgeon reviews), 3 external links redirect to generic pages (content drift), 16 unprocessed digest reviews (light backlog, new prefixes worth verifying), 7 tracker items with schema-drift output_file fields, spurious EXP-050 flag, persistent EXP-052 ID gap, and a dome-site WIN-057 number collision we don't fully cover. The previous run's 'fail' status is now resolved — decider must have run build.js publish between yesterday's report and this morning.
